questions.title: When does parent absorb child cost?
questions.context: Today src/runtime/coordinator.ts:787 dispatchDelegate emits `sub-run-completed` with `subResult` but does NOT update `totalCost` (only worker turns and plan-turn provider responses bump it). BUDGET-03 requires parent.accounting = parent's own provider calls + sum of children, recursive. The roll-up has to land somewhere.

options.label: Add at sub-run-completed emit
options.description: `totalCost = addCost(totalCost, subResult.cost)` immediately after a successful child returns, before the next plan turn. Single line change in dispatchDelegate; parent.totalCost is always current; final event already carries the rolled-up number.

options.label: Defer to a finalization pass
options.description: Walk events at run finalization and sum sub-run-completed.subResult.cost into parent's totalCost. Keeps live totalCost as 'parent-local'; downside: termination policies that read totalCost mid-run see local-only numbers.

options.label: Don't merge â€” expose accounting.children[]
options.description: Add a new public field `accounting.children: readonly RunAccounting[]` so callers compose totals themselves. Keeps cost.usd as 'parent provider calls only'. Public-surface addition; conflicts with the BUDGET-03 wording 'parent costUsd equals parent + sum of children'.

questions.title: Does a failed sub-run contribute cost?
questions.context: On `sub-run-failed`, the child threw â€” but real provider calls before the throw are real money. Today partialTrace is built but `subResult` is absent (only error + partialTrace). Decision affects whether dispatchDelegate must extract a cost from partialTrace or treat failed children as zero.

options.label: Yes â€” sum partialTrace cost into parent
options.description: Walk partialTrace.events for the last cost-bearing event (or accumulate provider events) and add to parent's totalCost. Most accurate to wallet reality; requires a small extractor in dispatchDelegate.

options.label: No â€” failed children contribute zero
options.description: Parent absorbs only successful children; failed children leave totalCost untouched. Simpler; under-reports real spend; surprising for users debugging a cancel/abort scenario.

options.label: Yes via a dedicated partialCost on the failed event
options.description: Lock `sub-run-failed.partialCost: CostSummary` as a public addition; roll-up reads from there. Public-surface delta; explicit; avoids walking partialTrace.events at runtime and again at replay.

questions.title: What does `final.cost` carry?
questions.context: src/runtime/defaults.ts:626 lastCostBearingEventCost reads `final.cost` (and agent-turn/broadcast/budget-stop). Replay-time `recomputeAccountingFromTrace` cross-checks parent's `finalOutput.cost` against this last event. If parent totalCost rolls up children, `final.cost` must be the rolled-up number for the existing replay invariant to keep holding.

options.label: Rolled-up â€” parent + children
options.description: Keep current invariant intact; replay stays a one-line check; downstream consumers reading `final.cost` see the wallet-truth.

options.label: Local-only on `final`, rolled-up on accounting
options.description: Splits semantics: final event = parent's own calls; accounting field = total. Requires a new replay-time reconciliation rule and breaks the existing invariant.

questions.title: Replay parity: enforce parent = local + sum(children)?
questions.context: src/runtime/defaults.ts:662 recomputeAccountingFromTrace currently computes parent's local accounting AND verifies each child's recorded accounting recursively, but does NOT verify that parent's recorded total equals local + sum(children). With BUDGET-03 landed, this becomes a verifiable invariant.

options.label: Enforce â€” parent total must equal local + sum(children)
options.description: Add a third comparison in recomputeAccountingFromTrace; mismatch throws DogpileError({ code: 'invalid-configuration', detail.reason: 'trace-accounting-mismatch', detail.kind: 'parent-rollup-drift' }). Strongest tamper detection; matches Phase 1 strictness.

options.label: Soft-warn â€” log on drift, don't throw
options.description: Misaligned with Phase 1's posture (D-10 chose throw). Inconsistent and confusing.

options.label: Skip â€” local + per-child checks are sufficient
options.description: Keep replay verification as-is. Risk: parent could record a tampered total that passes today but breaks BUDGET-03 in production.

questions.title: Token roll-up: same path as USD?
questions.context: RECOMPUTE_FIELD_ORDER (src/runtime/defaults.ts:553) lists eight numeric fields including cost.inputTokens/outputTokens/totalTokens and usage.* parallels. BUDGET-03 mentions cost AND tokens; tokens should roll up wherever USD does, with the same semantics.

options.label: Lock in parallel â€” tokens roll up identically
options.description: addCost already sums all fields; this is essentially a confirmation. Test: lock with one shared rollup test parameterized over the eight fields.

options.label: Tokens stay local, only USD rolls up
options.description: Asymmetric and surprising; would break consumers using usage.totalTokens for billing. Not recommended.

questions.title: Roll-up location: coordinator-only or engine-level?
questions.context: Only `coordinator` can emit delegate decisions today. dispatchDelegate lives in coordinator.ts. But `recomputeAccountingFromTrace` is engine-level (defaults.ts) and walks any trace. The live roll-up could mirror that engine-level shape.

options.label: Coordinator-only (in dispatchDelegate)
options.description: Simplest; localizes the change; matches where sub-run dispatch already lives. Other protocols never delegate, so they never need it.

options.label: Engine-level helper
options.description: Add `accumulateSubRunCost` in defaults.ts; coordinator imports it. Future protocols can reuse if delegate spreads. Trivial extra abstraction.

questions.title: How does parent abort reach the child?
questions.context: src/runtime/coordinator.ts:878 already passes `signal: options.signal` into childOptions verbatim. The child engine (src/runtime/engine.ts:341) creates its own AbortController and wires the parent signal via wireCallerAbortSignal. So 'parent abort â†’ child abort' already works structurally. Question is whether the test surface needs more, or whether we want a child-derived controller for richer telemetry.

options.label: Keep current â€” pass parent signal verbatim
options.description: Child engine already chains its abortController to parent via wireCallerAbortSignal. Add tests in src/tests/cancellation-contract.test.ts that exercise: (1) parent.signal.abort() â†’ all in-flight children throw `aborted`, (2) the abort reason propagates as cause.

options.label: Derive a child AbortController in dispatchDelegate
options.description: `childController = new AbortController(); options.signal?.addEventListener('abort', () => childController.abort(reason));` Lets parent signal multiple unrelated runs without entanglement. Slight allocation overhead per child; cleaner ownership story.

options.label: Per-child controller AND keep parent signal
options.description: Best telemetry for Phase 4 streaming, but double-wires the abort path. Defer until Phase 4 needs the per-child handle.

questions.title: What error code does an aborted child throw?
questions.context: BUDGET-01 says 'child runs surface DogpileError({ code: "aborted" })'. Today the child engine creates the abort error via createAbortErrorFromSignal (src/runtime/cancellation.ts:22) which preserves the parent's reason. ERROR-03 (Phase 4 scope) says child timeouts surface as `provider-timeout` while parent timeouts surface as `aborted` with `detail.reason: "timeout"`.

options.label: Plain `aborted` â€” defer detail.reason to Phase 4
options.description: Phase 2 only requires the code; ERROR-03's detail.reason: 'timeout' enrichment lands in Phase 4 with the rest of error escalation. Keeps Phase 2 surface minimal.

options.label: `aborted` + detail.reason: 'parent-aborted' or 'timeout' now
options.description: Lock the detail discriminator now so Phase 4 doesn't have to redo the abort path. Two new detail.reason variants to cover: parent-aborted (signal explicit) and timeout (parent deadline). Slight Phase 4 forward-compat work in Phase 2.

options.label: Custom code per source â€” `aborted` vs `parent-aborted`
options.description: Public surface change to error codes. Most expressive; most expensive. Conflicts with BUDGET-01's literal wording.

questions.title: Aborted child â†’ sub-run-failed.partialTrace shape
questions.context: src/runtime/coordinator.ts:896 buildPartialTrace runs from the locally tee'd childEvents buffer regardless of error type. For aborted children, the buffer may be small (no model calls fired yet) or sizeable (mid-flight abort during a turn). BUDGET-01 wants the abort observable on the parent.

options.label: Always emit sub-run-failed with whatever partialTrace exists
options.description: Current code path; keeps the failed event uniform regardless of cause; consumers can sniff `error.code === 'aborted'` for branch logic.

options.label: Suppress sub-run-failed when parent itself was aborted
options.description: Avoids redundant noise (parent is exiting anyway); but loses observability â€” replay can't tell which children completed vs which were torn down.

options.label: Emit sub-run-failed but trim partialTrace to {events: [], finalOutput: â€¦}
options.description: Smaller traces for the abort path; loses provenance (e.g., did the child make any provider calls before tear-down?).

questions.title: Parent aborts after sub-run already completed
questions.context: Race: child returns successfully, sub-run-completed pushed to parent events, then parent.signal aborts before the next plan turn fires. Today the abort throws on the next throwIfAborted (line 419/498). The completed event stays on the trace.

options.label: No-op â€” keep completed event, propagate abort on next yield
options.description: Current behavior; replay still works (child completed, parent aborted). No new event types needed.

options.label: Emit a parent-level `aborted` marker event after the sub-run-completed
options.description: Adds a new event variant; richer observability; public-surface addition. Probably overkill for Phase 2.

questions.title: What is the 'parent's remaining time'?
questions.context: src/runtime/coordinator.ts:800 currently reads `parentTimeoutMs = options.budget?.timeoutMs` â€” the static cap value. BUDGET-02 wording is 'children inherit the parent's remaining time'. The static cap is wrong if the parent has been running for 8 of its 10 second budget and dispatches a child â€” current code gives the child the full 10s.

options.label: Compute remaining = deadline âˆ’ now
options.description: dispatchDelegate snapshots `parentDeadlineMs = startedAtMs + budget.timeoutMs` once at parent run start (or reads engine-tracked deadline); child gets `Math.max(0, parentDeadlineMs âˆ’ Date.now())`. Correct to spec; needs deadline plumbed into dispatchDelegate options.

options.label: Keep static cap (current behavior)
options.description: Wrong per BUDGET-02 wording but simpler. Children running at the end of parent's window can blow past the parent's deadline before the parent's abortController fires (race-y but bounded by parent timeout firing the abort).

questions.title: Decision-time budget override that exceeds remaining
questions.context: src/runtime/coordinator.ts:810 currently THROWS invalid-configuration if `decisionTimeoutMs > remainingMs`. BUDGET-02 says 'per-decision budget overrides are honored but cannot exceed parent's remaining'. Three readings of 'cannot exceed': error / clamp / warn.

options.label: Error (current)
options.description: Loud failure; agent can't accidentally over-spend; matches Phase 1's strict posture. Risk: agents that emit a fixed budget never adapt to runtime drift.

options.label: Silently clamp to remaining
options.description: Charitable to agents; loses signal about agent miscalculation. Diverges from Phase 1's loud-failure design.

options.label: Clamp + emit warning event
options.description: Best of both: child gets a usable budget; trace records the clamp via a new `subRun.budgetClamped` event variant. Public-surface addition; matches the `subRun.concurrencyClamped` pattern from Phase 3.

questions.title: Phase-4 ERROR-03 timeout-vs-aborted distinction in Phase 2
questions.context: ERROR-03 (Phase 4 scope per ROADMAP) says child timeouts surface as `provider-timeout`, parent timeouts as `aborted` with `detail.reason: "timeout"`. Phase 2 builds the propagation path; Phase 4 builds the surfacing. Question: how much of the ERROR-03 reason-tagging do we lock in Phase 2?

options.label: Plumb the reason in Phase 2; lock the test in Phase 4
options.description: src/runtime/engine.ts:383 timeout setTimeout fires `abortController.abort(timeoutError)`; the timeoutError carries `code: 'timeout'`. Already discriminable today. Phase 2 only ensures the propagation works; Phase 4 owns the contract test.

options.label: Lock both now in Phase 2
options.description: Add detail.reason: 'timeout' on the parent-timeout abort path and add a contract test in src/tests/cancellation-contract.test.ts. Pulls in Phase 4 work; keeps the abort path coherent in one PR.

questions.title: Default child timeout when parent has no `timeoutMs`
questions.context: src/runtime/coordinator.ts:827 today: if parent has no timeoutMs and decision has no timeoutMs, child has no timeoutMs. Inherits the parent's uncapped posture.

options.label: Keep undefined (current)
options.description: Symmetric; Phase 1 D-12 already chose this. No behavior change.

options.label: Apply an engine-level default ceiling for sub-runs
options.description: E.g., `engine.defaultSubRunTimeoutMs`. Lock against accidental runaway children when parent forgot to set a budget. Public-surface addition.

questions.title: Parent termination evaluates parent-level events only â€” proof?
questions.context: Today src/runtime/coordinator.ts:136 `emit` pushes ONLY events the parent emits to parent's local `events`. The teedEmit in dispatchDelegate (line 848) pushes child events to the EXTERNAL stream callback only, NOT to parent's local events. So parent-level termination naturally sees only parent events. BUDGET-04 wants this locked.

options.label: Lock with a contract test in budget-first-stop.test.ts
options.description: Build a recursive scenario where a child emits 50 agent-turn events; assert parent's terminate.budget.maxIterations counts only parent events (sub-run-started/completed count as 1 each). Catches any future leak via teedEmit refactor.

options.label: Add a parent-events array invariant comment + smaller spy test
options.description: Lighter weight; documents the invariant in code; gives a smaller test.

questions.title: minTurns / minRounds floors per-instance
questions.context: src/runtime/termination.ts:445 protocolMinTurns reads from the protocol config of the running protocol-instance. Each child engine runs with its own protocol config (passed in childOptions) and its own transcript. Floors should naturally scope per-instance â€” but BUDGET-04 says lock it.

options.label: Test: parent has minTurns=3, child has minTurns=5; assert independence
options.description: Construct a coordinator parent with minTurns=3 and a delegated sequential child with minTurns=5; assert child runs â‰¥5 turns and parent exits at 3 once child returns. Place in budget-first-stop.test.ts or a new termination-recursive.test.ts.

options.label: Skip â€” implementation already gives this for free
options.description: Not running a test risks invisible regression if anyone refactors the protocol config plumbing. Not recommended.

questions.title: Do sub-run events count as parent termination iterations?
questions.context: `firstOf`/`convergence`/`judge` evaluate against `events` and `transcript`. Phase 1 D-18 added a synthetic transcript entry per completed sub-run. So `transcript.length` includes one entry per sub-run AND one per worker turn. budget.maxIterations reads `iteration` which is `transcript.length` in coordinator (line 431).

options.label: Lock current behavior â€” sub-run counts as 1 iteration
options.description: Each delegate counts as one parent iteration via the synthetic transcript entry. Matches D-18 design. Document with a contract test.

options.label: Exclude sub-run synthetic entries from iteration count
options.description: Filter transcript by role !== 'delegate-result' for iteration math. Cleaner separation; needs careful audit of every termination call site to swap iteration source.

questions.title: Test file organization for Phase 2
questions.context: Existing files: src/tests/cancellation-contract.test.ts, src/tests/budget-first-stop.test.ts, src/runtime/coordinator.test.ts. ROADMAP Phase 2 Key files lists those three plus termination.

options.label: Extend existing files
options.description: Add recursive-tree cases to cancellation-contract and budget-first-stop. Keeps the contract surface centralized; one place to find the canonical test for each public guarantee.

options.label: New file per concern
options.description: termination-recursive.test.ts, cost-rollup.test.ts, deadline-propagation.test.ts. More files, easier to grep, but fragments contract-test discoverability.

options.label: Hybrid
options.description: Contract guarantees â†’ existing files; deep cases (e.g., 4-level nesting cost roll-up) â†’ coordinator.test.ts as scenario tests.

questions.title: Public-surface deltas to lock now
questions.context: Phase 1 added: `delegate` decision variant, sub-run-started/completed/failed events, RunResult on subResult, partialTrace on failed, recursive flag on started, maxDepth options. CLAUDE.md mandates updating event-schema.test.ts, result-contract.test.ts, package-exports.test.ts, package.json, CHANGELOG.md together for any public-surface change.

options.label: No new public types â€” pure semantics
options.description: BUDGET-01..04 are all behavioral; no new fields/events needed if Q-02=a (failed cost from partialTrace) and Q-12=a (error on overshoot). Cheapest.

options.label: Add `subRun.budgetClamped` event (if Q-12=c)
options.description: New RunEvent variant; updates event-schema.test.ts + result-contract.test.ts + CHANGELOG. Keeps clamp visible in trace.

options.label: Add `partialCost` field on sub-run-failed (if Q-02=c)
options.description: Public surface change to the failed event; updates schema/contract/CHANGELOG; gives explicit cost capture without partialTrace walk.

options.label: Both â€” full observability surface
options.description: Both b and c. Largest surface delta; richest replay; biggest CHANGELOG entry.

questions.title: Plan granularity
questions.context: Phase 1 broke into 5 plans (decision union, event types, dispatch loop, maxDepth, replay). Phase 2 has 4 requirements (BUDGET-01..04) but they touch overlapping files (coordinator.ts, defaults.ts, cancellation.ts, termination.ts).

options.label: 4 plans, one per requirement
options.description: BUDGET-01 cancellation, BUDGET-02 timeout/deadline, BUDGET-03 cost roll-up, BUDGET-04 termination floors. Clean traceability; some shared churn on coordinator.ts dispatchDelegate across plans.

options.label: 3 plans grouped by surface
options.description: (1) abort + timeout propagation (BUDGET-01+02 share the abort/deadline path), (2) cost roll-up + replay parity (BUDGET-03), (3) termination floors lock (BUDGET-04). Fewer commits, less coordinator.ts churn collision.

options.label: 2 plans â€” propagation vs accounting
options.description: (1) Propagation: BUDGET-01+02+04, (2) Accounting: BUDGET-03 + replay-parity. Largest commits; risk of executor blowing context on a single plan.
